title: Hallucination Rates in Large Language Models: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Across Models, Domains, and Evaluation Methods
abstract: This study presents the first comprehensive quantitative meta-analysis of LLM hallucination rates, synthesizing 38 empirical studies published between 2022 and 2026. Following PRISMA 2020 guidelines, we estimated a pooled hallucination rate of 14.7% (95% CI: 11.2–18.9%) with substantial heterogeneity (I² = 94.3%). Subgroup analyses revealed significant variation across application domains, with medical applications exhibiting the highest rates (15.8%) and text summarization the lowest (8.4%). Model family comparisons indicated that Claude 3.x (8.7%) and GPT-4 class models (10.3%) achieved lower hallucination rates than smaller open-source alternatives (24.8% for 7–13B parameter models). Values marked ⁻ˢ are simulated for demonstration.
